title: शिव अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली
devanagariText:

ॐ हराय नमः

॥ इति श्री शिव अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली सम्पूर्ण ॥

|| Iti Shri Shiva Ashtottara Shatanamavali Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: शिव अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली भगवान शिव के 108 पवित्र नामों का संग्रह है। प्रत्येक नाम 'ॐ' से आरम्भ होता है और 'नमः' से समाप्त होता है। इन नामों में शिव के विभिन्न रूपों, गुणों और महिमा का वर्णन है - शिव (कल्याणकारी), महेश्वर (महान ईश्वर), शम्भु (सुख देने वाले), शंकर (शुभ करने वाले), मृत्युञ्जय (मृत्यु को जीतने वाले), महादेव (देवों के देव), सदाशिव (सदैव कल्याणकारी) आदि।
benefits: Reciting 108 names of Shiva bestows complete grace of Mahadeva
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins, sorrows, and obstacles from life
Invokes moksha and liberation from the cycle of birth and death
Used during Shiva Abhishek and Rudrabhishek puja
Particularly powerful on Mondays, Maha Shivaratri, and Pradosh
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Shiva, Shiva Ashtottara Shatanamavali (शिव अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) stands as a devotional recitation of 108 sacred names. Attributed to Shiva Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to remove all sins, sorrows, and obstacles from life, used during Shiva Abhishek and Rudrabhishek puja, and grant moksha and liberation from the cycle of birth and death. It is especially recommended on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri, Pradosh, and Shravan month for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 108 verses, it can be completed in approximately 15 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Ashtottara Shatanamavali in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Shiva Ashtottara Shatanamavali - 108 names of Lord Shiva in Sanskrit with transliteration. For Shiva Abhishek and daily worship.
